feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://www.healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: Period pain: Simple shopping swap for better pain relief, study reveals
entry.summary: Many women are buying less effective pain medication for period cramps, supermarket data suggests.

entry.title: Ebola cases in eastern Congo climb to 782 and deaths reach 181, authorities say
entry.summary: The Congolese Ministry of Health reports that confirmed Ebola cases in Congo have increased to 782, with 181 deaths. The outbreak, declared on May 15, is caused by the rare Bundibugyo virus, which lacks an approved vaccine or treatment. The situation is worse…

entry.title: This Painful Condition Impacts Women Way More Than Men, And You Need To Know About It
entry.summary: The condition is painful, limiting, and often mistaken for normal.

entry.title: Mind-Blowing Simulation Reveals What 36 Hours Of Fasting Really Does To Your Body
entry.summary: While the simulation video depicts the potential benefits of intermittent fasting, experts are not fully on board with the practice.

entry.title: Behind the Scenes at World Cup, Lots of Disease Busters
entry.summary: Officials will be on lookout for health threats during soccer tourney's mass gatherings

entry.title: Psilocybin Unlocks Lost Memories in an Alzheimer’s Patient
entry.summary: A new Alzheimer's case report reveals that a single dose of psilocybin temporarily restored speech and movement in an advanced dementia patient.

entry.title: An 88-Year-Old Woman Was Brought To My ER. When Her Family Told Me Why, I Was Stunned.
entry.summary: "I cleared my throat. 'So, I was wondering, why did you bring Maria to the hospital today?' Instantly all three sets of eyes filled with tears."

entry.title: World's most infectious disease case found in California after they passed through super spreader hubs
entry.summary: Anyone who was present at those sites during the specified time frames may have been exposed, officials said.

entry.title: Measles case found in California after they passed through San Francisco Airport
entry.summary: Anyone who was present at those sites during the specified time frames may have been exposed, officials said.

entry.title: Scientists Identify 2 Distinct Subtypes of Autism in The Brain
entry.summary: Autism is a complex condition that can affect different people in very different ways.

entry.title: 11 Foods Highest in Vitamin D
entry.summary: The sun isn't the only source of vitamin D. Foods like eggs and fortified milks can provide the nutrient, supporting bone density and immune function.

entry.title: My Wife’s Puritanical “Sex Rule” Is Really Putting a Damper Our Big Jamaican Vacation
entry.summary: Why is she like this?

entry.title: What Eating Sourdough Does to Your Blood Sugar, According to Nutritionists
entry.summary: Find out how eating sourdough impacts blood sugar compared to eating standard white bread. Experts say sourdough can cause a gentler rise in blood sugar due to the fermentation process used to make it. For optimal benefits, pair it with sources of protein, fa…

entry.title: This high-fat eating plan may offer a powerful way to shield the aging brain
entry.summary: The gut and brain are in constant conversation through a powerful biochemical signaling pathway. This two-way connection allows them to exchange signals that influence everything from digestion to emotional health, and studies suggest even the fate of neurode…

entry.title: The protein craze: Are high-protein diets and supplements putting your liver at risk?
entry.summary: Protein has become the star of the modern wellness movement. Walk through any supermarket aisle and it is hard to miss protein shakes, protein cookies, protein chips, protein cereals, and even protein-infused beverages. Social media feeds are packed with fitn…

entry.title: 7 Sneaky Signs Your Blood Sugar Is Too High, According to Dietitians
entry.summary: High blood sugar is a risk factor for prediabetes and diabetes. These are the warning signs dietitians want you to know.

entry.title: 5 Drinks With More Blood Pressure Benefits Than Green Tea
entry.summary: Dietitians share the best drinks for blood pressure support beyond green tea, including beet juice, hibiscus tea, pomegranate juice and more.

entry.title: Common supplement may accelerate memory loss from Alzheimer’s disease
entry.summary: Glucosamine is a popular arthritis remedy, but it may be dangerous for those with early memory loss. New research indicates this sugar molecule disrupts brain chemistry, accelerating both Alzheimer's disease progression and mortality rates.

entry.title: Struggling With Brain Fog And Fatigue? You Might Be Lacking This 1 Essential Mineral.
entry.summary: Treating the issue could help you feel much better, but it may take some self-advocacy.

entry.title: 5 Exercises You Should Do In The Morning, According To Experts
entry.summary: Feel like moving your body when you get out of bed? Fitness pros share the workout moves that are most effective in the a.m.

entry.title: 2 daylight raccoon attacks within hours have New Jersey town eyeing rabies pellets
entry.summary: Animal Control officers searching the area have not found evidence of the raccoon.

entry.title: Researchers are uncovering ADHD's links to these other health conditions
entry.summary: Attention-deficit/hyperactivity disorder, or ADHD, is one of the most common neurodevelopmental conditions in the United States, affecting an estimated 15.5...

entry.title: These longevity meal swaps may lower your biological age — while saving time and money
entry.summary: A new study suggests swapping some, but not all, of your meat for plant proteins can help improve longevity.

entry.title: Column | Six things I did to rid my yard of mosquitoes when nothing else worked
entry.summary: This neighborhood plan mimics ones used to eradicate the insects on islands. Could it work for my house?

entry.title: A single dose of psilocybin provides months of relief from chronic suicidal thoughts in new study
entry.summary: A recent study in The Journal of Clinical Psychiatry suggests that a single dose of psilocybin, paired with psychological support, may rapidly and safely reduce chronic suicidal thoughts in adults with severe, treatment-resistant depression.

entry.title: ADHD is linked to chronic pain and other conditions. Here’s what we know.
entry.summary: A growing body of evidence suggests that people with ADHD may be at risk for anxiety, disordered eating, migraines, long covid and other problems.

entry.title: The Best Morning Drink to Help You Poop (Besides Coffee)
entry.summary: Gut health experts share their favorite morning drink besides coffee to help you poop in the morning, along with other tips for supporting gut health and regularity.

entry.title: How a nutritionist hits her protein goal without tracking food or counting macros
entry.summary: According to Sophie Gastman, a registered nutritionist, you don't need to count your macros to eat enough protein. Instead, she keeps her kitchen stocked with certain essentials.

entry.title: Newton-Wellesley Hospital's brain tumor investigation: New info on the 11 diagnosed nurses
entry.summary: Newton-Wellesley Hospital’s brain tumor investigation: A new report on the 11 diagnosed nurses from the maternity wing

entry.title: 11 Massachusetts nurses were diagnosed with brain tumors: Is it a 'cancer cluster'?
entry.summary: 11 Massachusetts nurses on the same hospital floor were diagnosed with brain tumors: Is it a cancer cluster?

entry.title: The Pacemaker Patch
entry.summary: A pacemaker is implanted to send signals that regulate a patient’s heartbeat, and to do that, you need power. That means they require battery changes, and when the device in question happens …

entry.title: Sonoma County programs aim to address alarming rates of senior isolation
entry.summary: Sonoma County, which skews older than the state or nation, has a litany of programs meant to tackle alarming rates of isolation, loneliness and suicide among seniors living locally.

entry.title: Latest CDC data puts Pocatello among nation's top COVID wastewater hot spots
entry.summary: The COVID-19 pandemic may feel like a distant memory for most Pocatello residents, but something unusual is showing up in the city's wastewater — and public health officials are paying

entry.title: What to know about alpha-gal syndrome, the life-threatening meat allergy caused by tick bites
entry.summary: Health officials are raising awareness about alpha-gal syndrome, a meat allergy triggered by tick bites. This condition is on the rise. Symptoms include hives, diarrhea and itchiness after eating meat or dairy. The allergy doesn’t impact consumption of seafoo…

entry.title: South Bay resident with measles visited SFO airport, several other San Jose locations
entry.summary: Members of the public who visited the sites at those times on June 8 may be at risk of developing measles.

entry.title: The fight over America's vaccine future
entry.summary: A pivotal series of decisions in the next few months will determine the shape of U.S. vaccine policy for years to come.

entry.title: Dogs and humans are more alike than we thought, study finds
entry.summary: The same biological signals that help predict lifespan in humans also appear in dogs, according to new research from the Dog Aging Project—a finding that could help scientists better understand aging in both species.

entry.title: Anaphylaxis concerns for Cardiff boy, 10, with 20 allergies
entry.summary: Yann Jennings, 10, has allergies including seafood, cats and coconut, but can now eat some nuts daily.

entry.title: 445 birds euthanized due to bird flu outbreak at Rhode Island market
entry.summary: The Rhode Island Department of Health and Rhode Island Department of Environmental Management said that 445 birds at a bird market in Providence were euthanized

entry.title: Paper or pixel? Science settles divisive debate over best way to read — real books or screens
entry.summary: Here’s the word on the best medium for reading

entry.title: Older adults who took a weekly fifteen-minute walk for eight weeks felt measurably better, but the difference came down to one instruction: those told to seek out small moments of awe reported more gratitude and compassion and less daily distress
entry.summary: “Experiencing awe is such a simple practice, just taking a moment to look out the window or pausing to consider the technological marvels that surround us, and we now show it can have measurable effects on our emotional well-being,” said Virginia Sturm, an as…

entry.title: Testosterone is being overprescribed to men — here's who should take it, and when it backfires
entry.summary: Getting testosterone therapy without the right medical care is a growing problem. Here's how to know if you need TRT, and what the risks are.

entry.title: Vitamin C levels in blood plasma linked with brain connectivity and volume in older adults
entry.summary: A study of 2,044 older Japanese adults found that those with lower vitamin C levels in their blood plasma tended to have a lower volume of gray matter in their brains, as well as lower connectivity among a collection of brain regions known as the default mode…

entry.title: Deadly dog virus is spreading across Arizona. Veterinarians say it's preventable.
entry.summary: A deadly dog virus is spreading rapidly across Arizona, with cases up 250% compared to the same time last year, and an emergency veterinarian is warning...

entry.title: Afternoon Habits That Can Quietly Work Against Heart Health
entry.summary: Find out which afternoon choices cardiologists say can have a lasting impact on heart health.

entry.title: Global HIV prevention declined drastically after Trump aid cuts, U.N. finds
entry.summary: Cuts from the U.S. and others have hit hard, with the number of people receiving PrEP dropping by 38% from 2024 to 2025, according to data from 62 countries.

entry.title: 7-Day No-Sugar, High-Protein Meal Plan for High Blood Pressure, Created by a Dietitian
entry.summary: Follow this 7-day high-protein meal plan for a week of no-sugar-added meals and snacks tailored to improve your blood pressure.

entry.title: Sugar-free, low-fat diet tied to insulin resistance in mouse study
entry.summary: Mice kept on a sugar-free, low-fat diet developed insulin resistance and other metabolic changes in a recent study.

entry.title: Millennials Like Me Were Sold a Fantasy About Weed. Now Researchers Have a Warning.
entry.summary: This wasn’t at all what I imagined when I first lit up.

entry.title: Ozempic Users Are Skimping Out on Exercise, Study Finds
entry.summary: People's daily steps might drop after starting a GLP-1 prescription, new research suggests.
